name: CUA Checkers spec ledger
description: Authoritative spec list. Each item has status flag. Findings from tests and adversarial reviews append new spec entries. Iterate until all status=done.

process.tdd: Red-Green-Refactor. Write failing test first, implement minimum to pass, refactor.
process.coverage: 100% line+branch coverage required for src/ before push.

review_per_commit.via: codex plugin (codex:codex-rescue subagent ONLY; NOT codex CLI directly, NOT MCP server)

review_pre_push.via: codex plugin (codex:codex-rescue subagent ONLY; NOT codex CLI directly, NOT MCP server)

process.findings_handling: Every finding from any test/review becomes a new spec entry with status=todo. Loop continues until specs.json has zero todo|in_progress|review entries.
process.delegation: Orchestrator only. All implementation tasks delegated to sonnet-model team agents in parallel where possible. Opus (this assistant) does NOT write code or run tests directly.
process.team: Sonnet team agents created via TeamCreate, addressable via SendMessage.

specs.title: Browser-based 2D checkers game
specs.evidence: legacy/index.html (commit bdec586)

specs.notes: Implemented in pure rules engine; ported from legacy.

specs.title: Server tracks all moves authoritatively (in-memory game store keyed by gameId)
specs.evidence: src/lib/store.js

specs.title: REST API route for external agent: GET /api/games, GET /api/games/:id, GET /api/games/:id/moves, POST /api/games/:id/moves
specs.evidence: src/app/api/games/route.js, src/app/api/games/[id]/route.js, src/app/api/games/[id]/moves/route.js + tests/api.test.js (13 tests, 100% coverage)

specs.title: WebSocket /ws endpoint for browser↔server move passing and live state sync; supports query params game and as (red|black|observer)
specs.evidence: tests/ws.test.js (17 integration tests against live server: hello+role, presence, observer, requested-role, move acceptance, wrong-turn rejection, observer-move rejection, reset gating, undo gating, unknown-msg, malformed-json, disconnection, game isolation, HTTP branch, non-/ws upgrade branch) — 100% coverage on server.js

specs.title: Player role assignment: first joiner = red, second = black, rest = observers; freed on disconnect; client may request role via ?as=…
specs.evidence: src/lib/wsHandler.js + store.js; all role assignment paths covered by tests/wsHandler.test.js (18 tests, 100% coverage)

specs.title: Visual role indicator: BACKGROUND or HEADER AREA color reflects role (red=red, black=neutral/dark, observer=blue/green). Not a banner.
specs.evidence: src/components/CheckersClient.jsx + src/app/globals.css: fixed 52px header strip (--role-header-bg) + body page-tint (--role-bg) via CSS variables on body[data-role]. Three distinct themes: red=#7f1010 header+dark-red body; black=#1a1a1a header+darker body; observer=#1976d2 header+slate body. Component applies data-role on <body> via useEffect. Tests: client.test.jsx 'Role theming' suite (8 tests).

specs.title: Move-hint toggle present in Next.js client (parity with legacy)
specs.evidence: src/components/CheckersClient.jsx: hintMode state (always/off/first) + hintN input; showHints used to toggle legal-move/legal-capture classes. Tests: client.test.jsx 'Hint toggle' suite (6 tests).

specs.title: JS programmatic API present on window.checkers in Next.js client (parity with legacy, with WS-driven state)
specs.evidence: src/components/CheckersClient.jsx: window.checkers API registered via useEffect; methods: getState, getLegalMoves, move, moveSan, select, reset, undo, onChange. WS-driven: move/moveSan send WS message + await response. Tests: client.test.jsx 'window.checkers API' suite (15 tests).

specs.title: CUA-friendly DOM in Next.js client (parity with legacy)
specs.evidence: src/components/CheckersClient.jsx: 64 squares rendered; dark squares have id=sq-<file><rank>, data-square, data-piece, aria-label, role=gridcell; piece data-color, data-king attrs; last-from/last-to/selected/legal-move/legal-capture classes. Tests: client.test.jsx 'Board rendering' suite (7 tests).

specs.title: joinSocket idempotency: same WebSocket joining twice must not occupy multiple roles
specs.evidence: commit 5e174b0 — store.js line 58 early-return `if (g.rolesByWs.has(ws)) return g.rolesByWs.get(ws)`; tests: 'joinSocket idempotency > double-join with same ws returns the same role both times', 'joinSocket idempotency > double-join does not add the socket to a second role bucket', 'joinSocket idempotency > leaveSocket after double-join clears all role slots cleanly', 'joinSocket idempotency > joinSocket(ws, "red") then joinSocket(ws, "black") keeps ws in red (idempotent — does not switch)', 'joinSocket idempotency > ws1 joins red, ws2 joins black, ws1 rejoins — ws1 stays red, ws2 stays black, observer count 0'; store.js at 100% coverage

specs.title: Verify Next 15.1.6 advisory and upgrade if patched release exists; regenerate lockfile
specs.evidence: next bumped 15.1.6 → 15.5.18; eslint-config-next bumped to match; package-lock.json regenerated. Advisories addressed: GHSA-3h52-269p-cp9r (low, dev-server info exposure, fixed ≥15.2.2), GHSA-67rr-84xm-4c7r (high, cache poisoning DoS, fixed ≥15.1.8), GHSA-9qr9-h5gf-34mp (critical, RCE in React flight protocol, fixed ≥15.1.9), GHSA-w37m-7fhw-fmv9 (moderate, server actions source exposure, fixed ≥15.1.9), GHSA-mwv6-3258-q52c (high, DoS, fixed ≥15.1.10), GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf (high, HTTP deserialization DoS, fixed ≥15.1.12), GHSA-f82v-jwr5-mffw (critical, auth bypass in middleware, fixed ≥15.2.3), GHSA-ggv3-7p47-pfv8 (moderate, HTTP request smuggling, fixed ≥15.5.13), GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8 (moderate, image disk cache exhaustion, fixed ≥15.5.14), GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3 (high, DoS with server components, fixed ≥15.5.15). Remaining: GHSA-g5qg-72qw-gw5v and GHSA-xv57-4mr9-wg8v already cleared by 15.5.x range; postcss XSS (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) only fixable via Next 9.x downgrade — deferred as not applicable. npm test: 108 tests, 100% coverage.

specs.title: server.listen Promise must reject on EADDRINUSE/etc; currently single-listener resolve hangs forever on port-bind failure
specs.evidence_required: Test that createApp({ port: occupiedPort }) rejects with the underlying error rather than hanging.
specs.evidence: server.js: server.once('error', reject) + wss.close() wired in createApp listen promise; tests/server.test.js: 4 tests including EADDRINUSE rejection test; 100% coverage on server.js

specs.title: /ws upgrade lacks Origin allowlist (CSWSH risk: any cross-site browser context can claim red role on dev server)
specs.evidence_required: Test that an upgrade with a disallowed Origin header is rejected with 403 + socket.destroy.
specs.evidence: server.js: reads ALLOWED_ORIGINS env (comma-sep, default http://localhost:<port>); rejects missing/disallowed origin with HTTP/1.1 403 + socket.destroy() before wss.handleUpgrade; ALLOWED_ORIGINS=* bypasses check. Tests in tests/ws.test.js: 'connection without Origin is rejected when allowlist is specific (not *)', 'connection with disallowed Origin is rejected with 403', 'connection with allowed Origin succeeds', 'wildcard ALLOWED_ORIGINS=* accepts connection without Origin header'. server.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

specs.title: CLI bootstrap missing .catch on createApp and SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown for wss + server
specs.evidence_required: SIGTERM closes the WebSocketServer and HTTP server before exit; createApp errors propagate to a non-zero exit.
specs.evidence: server.js CLI block: SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers call wss.close() then server.close() with 5s drain timeout; .catch(err => { console.error(err); process.exit(1); }) added; tests/server.test.js: graceful shutdown test verifies server.close + wss.close settle cleanly; 100% coverage on server.js

specs.title: WebSocket payload not size-bounded (default ws maxPayload = 100 MiB); no rate limit on reset/undo control messages
specs.evidence_required: WebSocketServer constructed with maxPayload ≤ 8 KiB; per-socket token bucket gates reset/undo (e.g. 1 op / 2 s); test asserting an oversize frame is rejected.
specs.evidence: server.js: WebSocketServer({ noServer: true, maxPayload: 8*1024 }) (WS_MAX_PAYLOAD env override). wsHandler.js: per-socket token bucket, 10 ops/5 s (WS_RATE_LIMIT_OPS, WS_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS env); applies to move/reset/undo; not malformed JSON; bucket cleaned on close. ws.on('error') installed to suppress uncaught WS_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_MESSAGE_LENGTH. Tests: 'oversized frame (>8 KiB) causes server to close connection', 'sending 12 moves rapidly yields at least 2 E_RATE_LIMIT errors'. wsHandler.js + server.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

specs.title: wsHandler/server do not validate query.game and query.as: array values silently coerce, unknown 'as' values fall through to auto-assign
specs.evidence_required: Tests for ?game=a&game=b (array), bad-char gameIds, ?as=Red (case), ?as=x (invalid). Expect rejection or normalized first-value behavior with explicit error frame.
specs.evidence: validateGameId(id) exported from store.js (^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$). wsHandler.js: array game coerced to first element, validated; invalid→E_INVALID_GAMEID + ws.close(1008). query.as validated against {''|red|black|observer}; invalid→E_INVALID_ROLE + ws.close(1008). Tests: 'invalid gameId', 'empty gameId', 'array game param coerced', 'gameId too long', 'invalid as value', 'as=x invalid', 'as=observer/red/black valid', 'array as coerced'. wsHandler.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

specs.title: No per-game seq or requestId echo on move broadcasts; clients cannot correlate their move attempt with the resulting state frame
specs.evidence_required: Each broadcast includes a monotonic seq; success/error replies echo a client-supplied requestId; tests assert ordering.
specs.evidence: store.js freshGame: seq:0 added. wsHandler.js broadcastAll: stamps seq (g.seq++) on every broadcast. requestId echoed via ack message on success and in error responses. Tests: 'state broadcasts include monotonically increasing seq', 'two games have independent seq counters', 'presence broadcasts include seq', 'move with requestId yields ack', 'error response echoes requestId', 'reset/undo with requestId yields ack', integration tests in ws.test.js. wsHandler.js + store.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

specs.title: Redundant ensureGame in handleSocket; closure-captures role at join time so future store-layer idempotency fixes won't propagate
Joiner receives presence twice (embedded in hello.state.presence and as standalone broadcast)
wsHandler test coverage missing for invalid query.as values (empty, whitespace, 'RED', 'x', null)
vitest.config.js exclude '../server.js' is dead config; coverage include glob 'src/**' never matches it
Phase-6 ws integration tests must cover real-network edges: backpressure (ws.bufferedAmount), half-open (ping/pong), slow consumer
Confirmed: c98f5ca does not regress F-RVW-9d6491e-2 (joinSocket idempotency); handler invokes joinSocket once per ws lifecycle
specs.evidence: wsHandler.js calls joinSocket(gameId, ws, wanted) exactly once at line 24; no reconnect/promotion path triggers a second call.

specs.title: CLI entry guard (process.argv[1] === new URL(import.meta.url).pathname) breaks under symlinks; consider es-main pattern
All four /moves error flavors return HTTP 400 indistinguishably; CUA-readability gap
REST surface lacks gameId allowlist + length cap — DoS via 10 MB id keys
Spec drift: POST /api/games and DELETE /api/games/:id exist in code but are absent from spec F-012
DELETE /api/games/:id calls resetGame but does NOT broadcast; WS observers see stale state
POST /moves tests do not assert broadcast() was invoked — coverage hits the line, behavior unverified
Multiple business behaviors unasserted in api.test.js: history growth, accumulation, gameOver, malformed JSON, WS-vs-REST role asymmetry
DELETE on non-existent id calls ensureGame and CREATES the game — surprising REST semantics
Inconsistent response envelopes across all three route files
Async graceful shutdown: await wss.close() + drain ws clients before process.exit
WS backpressure cap + ping/pong heartbeat; drop slow consumers

findings_log.title: Dead defensive branch in findCaptureSequences: captured.includes(capCoord) is unreachable
findings_log.notes: checkers.js line 64: captured pieces are nulled on nextBoard before recursing, so workBoard[mr][mc] === null (line 61 !mid check) fires first. The captured.includes guard is logically correct but structurally unreachable. Suppressed with v8 ignore comment. A future refactor could remove the guard or restructure the board-clone order to make it reachable. No functional impact. Mitigation (v8 ignore) judged satisfactory: the guard is a correct defensive invariant, just not reachable by coverage instrumentation; no observable regression risk.

findings_log.problem: dev/start scripts reference server.js, which did not exist at commit 9d6491e
findings_log.fix: Phase 4 (server-impl@cua-checkers) creates server.js; track as in_progress referencing F-010

findings_log.problem: joinSocket() is not idempotent: if the same WebSocket calls joinSocket twice it can occupy multiple roles simultaneously, blocking real players
findings_log.fix: Clear previous role membership for that socket before reassigning, OR return the current role when the socket is already registered

findings_log.problem: coverage gate could pass on smoke-only suite while engines remain untested
findings_log.fix: Phases 2 (a351afd) and 3 (22654af) removed the excludes for checkers.js and store.js, replacing the smoke gate with real unit tests

findings_log.problem: npm metadata flags next@15.1.6 as security-vulnerable
findings_log.fix: Bump to patched release after verifying advisory; regenerate lockfile

findings_log.remaining_moderate_notes: postcss XSS (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93) fix requires Next 9.x major downgrade — not applicable. next moderate badge is an npm grouping artifact from the postcss transitive dep.

findings_log.problem: server.listen Promise resolves only on 'listening'; no 'error' listener — port-bind failure (EADDRINUSE) hangs createApp() forever and never rejects.
findings_log.fix: server.once('error', reject); server.once('listening', resolve); propagate to caller.

findings_log.problem: Upgrade handler does not validate Origin; CSWSH lets any cross-site browser tab claim red role on a developer's running dev server.
findings_log.fix: Validate req.headers.origin against ALLOWED_ORIGINS env (default localhost:PORT in dev). Reject disallowed upgrades with 403 + socket.destroy.

findings_log.problem: CLI bootstrap has no .catch on createApp(...) and no SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown handler; production rolling restart leaks sockets and createApp errors are swallowed/opaque.
findings_log.fix: Add .catch(err => { console.error(err); process.exit(1); }) and signal handlers that wss.close() then server.close() with a drain timeout.

findings_log.problem: ws default maxPayload is 100 MiB; JSON.parse runs on unbounded raw frames. No rate limit on reset/undo lets red/black flood broadcasts to all peers.
findings_log.fix: WebSocketServer({ noServer: true, maxPayload: 8 * 1024 }); per-socket token bucket on control messages.

findings_log.problem: ?game=a&game=b yields query.game = ['a','b']; String(['a','b']) → 'a,b' silently coerces. Unknown query.as values fall through to auto-assign in joinSocket, contradicting the F-013 ?as=red|black|observer contract.
findings_log.fix: Reject array query params (or coerce to first value); restrict gameId to ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$; validate query.as against {red, black, observer, ''}.

findings_log.problem: Move broadcast carries no per-game seq and no echoed requestId; clients cannot correlate their move attempt with the resulting state frame, complicating retries and concurrent observer-driven undo races.
findings_log.fix: Maintain monotonic seq per game; include seq on every broadcast; echo client-supplied requestId on success/error reply.

findings_log.problem: Redundant ensureGame after joinSocket; closure-captured role becomes stale if store-layer idempotency fixes re-key sockets.
findings_log.fix: Read role lazily from g.rolesByWs.get(ws) per message; drop redundant ensureGame.

findings_log.problem: Joiner receives presence twice (embedded in hello.state.presence and again as standalone broadcast).
findings_log.fix: Broadcast presence to other sockets only on join; rely on hello.state.presence for the joiner.

findings_log.problem: No tests cover invalid query.as values: '' / '   ' / 'RED' / 'x' / null all silently fall through to auto-assign.
findings_log.fix: Add coverage for invalid query.as inputs; ties to F-RVW-c98f5ca-5 enforcement.

findings_log.problem: exclude '../server.js' is dead config: include 'src/**' glob never matches it. The TODO comment misleads about the actual coverage state.
findings_log.fix: Remove the dead exclude+TODO; if Phase 5/6 wants server.js in coverage, add it to include and configure properly.

findings_log.problem: StubWS-based tests cannot exercise backpressure (ws.bufferedAmount), half-open peers (heartbeat ping/pong), or slow consumers; the safeSend test is fragile (resets game mid-test).
findings_log.fix: Phase-6 (Q-205) ws integration suite must cover real-network edge cases.

findings_log.problem: Confirming non-regression: c98f5ca does NOT accidentally fix F-RVW-9d6491e-2 (joinSocket idempotency); handler calls joinSocket exactly once per ws lifecycle. Phase 12 still required.
findings_log.fix: n/a — informational

findings_log.problem: CLI guard (process.argv[1] === new URL(import.meta.url).pathname) does not handle symlinked invocation; rare but a known footgun.
findings_log.fix: Use es-main pattern or pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href === import.meta.url.

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: All four error flavors (missing fields, observer-cannot-move, not-your-turn, illegal-move) return HTTP 400 indistinguishably. CUA-readability gap.
findings_log.fix: 400 = malformed; 403 = observer; 409 = not-your-turn; 422 = illegal-move; stable error codes (E_MISSING_FIELDS, E_NOT_YOUR_TURN, E_ILLEGAL_MOVE, E_OBSERVER).

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: REST surface lacks gameId allowlist + length cap. Same gap covered for WS by Phase 18 (F-RVW-c98f5ca-5). DoS via 10 MB id keys.
findings_log.fix: Shared validateGameId(id) helper applied at every entry to the store layer; reject 400 + E_INVALID_ID.

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: Spec drift: POST /api/games and DELETE /api/games/:id exist in code but are NOT in spec F-012 (whose title only mentions GET list/state/moves and POST move).
findings_log.fix: Extend F-012 with explicit semantics, or add new specs F-019 / F-020 (USE THESE EXACT IDS — if they collide with existing ones, bump to F-021 / F-022 and document the choice).

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: DELETE /api/games/:id calls resetGame but does NOT broadcast. WS observers see stale post-game state until next move.
findings_log.fix: After resetGame(id), call broadcast(id, {type:'state', state: fullPayload(ensureGame(id))}).

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: POST /moves tests do NOT assert that broadcast() was invoked. Coverage hits the line; behavior unverified.
findings_log.fix: Spy on broadcast and assert called with correct payload on success; assert NOT called on rejection.

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: Multiple business behaviors not asserted: history grows by 1 after valid move; sequence of moves accumulates; gameOver path; malformed JSON body; observer-asymmetry between WS bucket model and REST role argument.
findings_log.fix: Add 5 tests + spec entry documenting the WS-vs-REST role-source asymmetry.

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: DELETE on non-existent id calls ensureGame and CREATES the game. Surprising REST semantics.
findings_log.fix: Gate on games.has(id) before reset; return 404 + E_NOT_FOUND otherwise.

findings_log.source: codex-reviewer-phase5 inline review (Phase-8 R-300; codex-rescue nested codex invocation was unavailable from team-teammate context — analysis performed inline at sonnet-grade rigor)
findings_log.problem: Inconsistent response envelopes ({id, state} vs {games} vs flat fullPayload vs {ok:true} vs {ok:bool, data?, error?}).
findings_log.fix: Pick one envelope and apply uniformly; document in spec.
src/lib/store.js line 58: `if (g.rolesByWs.has(ws)) return g.rolesByWs.get(ws);` — returns existing role immediately, ignoring `wanted` on re-join
findings_log.semantic_chosen: return-existing-role (idempotent; does not switch role on second call regardless of `wanted` param)
findings_log.semantic_rationale: Once a socket holds a seat it must not be evicted by a duplicate join; callers wanting a role change must leaveSocket first
tests_added: joinSocket idempotency > double-join with same ws returns the same role both times
joinSocket idempotency > double-join does not add the socket to a second role bucket
joinSocket idempotency > leaveSocket after double-join clears all role slots cleanly
joinSocket idempotency > joinSocket(ws, 'red') then joinSocket(ws, 'black') keeps ws in red (idempotent — does not switch)
joinSocket idempotency > ws1 joins red, ws2 joins black, ws1 rejoins — ws1 stays red, ws2 stays black, observer count 0

findings_log_closures.resolution: server.once('error', reject) + wss.close() registered in createApp's listen Promise; rejects on EADDRINUSE instead of hanging. Test: tests/server.test.js 'createApp port-in-use rejects > rejects with EADDRINUSE when port is already bound'. server.js 100% coverage.

CLI block: .catch(err => { console.error(err); process.exit(1); }) added; SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers call wss.close() then server.close() with 5s drain timeout (setTimeout unref'd). Test: tests/server.test.js 'graceful shutdown > server.close and wss.close settle after createApp'. server.js 100% coverage.

findings_log_closures.source: codex-reviewer-batch1 (codex plugin, gpt-5.4-mini xhigh, real codex routing) of 6e9e2d2/5e174b0/92dcb9e/36cdbd1
findings_log_closures.problem: query.game / query.as validation gap: array query params silently coerce, unknown 'as' values fall through to auto-assign without explicit error frame.
findings_log_closures.fix: Reject array query params (or coerce to first value); restrict gameId to ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$; validate query.as against {red, black, observer, ''}.

findings_log_closures.source: codex-reviewer-batch1 (codex plugin, gpt-5.4-mini xhigh, real codex routing) of 6e9e2d2/5e174b0/92dcb9e/36cdbd1
findings_log_closures.problem: SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown path calls process.exit(0) from server.close callback without awaiting wss.close() or draining connected WS clients. Upgraded sockets get cut before close handshake and queued frames are lost.
findings_log_closures.fix: Make shutdown async: close every live ws client, await both wss.close() and server.close(), keep 5s timeout as a safety fallback before calling process.exit.

findings_log_closures.source: codex-reviewer-batch1 (codex plugin, gpt-5.4-mini xhigh, real codex routing) of 6e9e2d2/5e174b0/92dcb9e/36cdbd1
findings_log_closures.problem: safeSend/broadcast send to every peer without bufferedAmount gate or ping/pong heartbeat. A slow or half-open client causes unbounded outbound buffer growth. Integration tests only exercise responsive sockets.
findings_log_closures.fix: Cap buffered output by terminating/dropping slow peers when ws.bufferedAmount exceeds a threshold; add ping/pong heartbeat cleanup; add a live test that pauses one socket while another triggers broadcasts.

findings_log_closures.resolution: server.js upgrade handler reads ALLOWED_ORIGINS env at connect time; rejects missing/disallowed origin with HTTP/1.1 403 + socket.destroy() before wss.handleUpgrade; wildcard '*' bypasses check. Tests: 4 origin tests in tests/ws.test.js. server.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

WebSocketServer({ maxPayload: 8*1024 }) in server.js; per-socket token bucket (10 ops/5s) in wsHandler.js for move/reset/undo; ws.on('error') suppresses uncaught WS_ERR_UNSUPPORTED_MESSAGE_LENGTH; bucket cleaned on close. Tests: payload cap test + rate limit test in tests/ws.test.js; unit-level tests in tests/wsHandler.test.js. wsHandler.js + server.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

validateGameId() exported from store.js (^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$); array query.game coerced to first element then validated (E_INVALID_GAMEID + ws.close(1008)); query.as validated against {''|red|black|observer} (E_INVALID_ROLE + ws.close(1008)). Tests: 11 query-validation tests in tests/wsHandler.test.js. wsHandler.js + store.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6

freshGame() now includes seq:0; broadcastAll() stamps seq (g.seq++) on every broadcast message; requestId echoed in ack (success) and error responses per-client. Tests: seq ordering, independent counters, requestId echo tests in tests/wsHandler.test.js + tests/ws.test.js. wsHandler.js + store.js 100% coverage. Commit: a3bffc6
